zh.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington於1440年出生在英國藍卡爾法利頓。她於1464年與Thomas Haworth of Farrington在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington于1440年出生在英国藍卡爾法利頓。她于1464年与Thomas Haworth of Farrington在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Farrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Farrington in Lancashire, but perhaps occasionally from Farrington Gurney in Somerset. Both are named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The surname also probably reached America via Ireland, where it is recorded as early as the 14th century.

variants.name: Fearrington
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Farrington  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: "Haworth"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Haworth (Yorkshire), probably from Old English haga ‘hedge, enclosure’ + worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Howarth
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Howarth in Rochdale (Lancashire), possibly from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + worth ‘enclosure’. The name may have been confused with Haworth .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington was born in 1440, in Farington, Lancashire, England. She married Thomas Haworth of Farrington in 1464, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington est née en 1440, à Farington, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Thomas Haworth of Farrington en 1464, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington wurde 1440 in Farington, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat 1464 in Lancashire, England Thomas Haworth of Farrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington è nata il nel 1440 nel Farington, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato Thomas Haworth of Farrington nel 1464, nel Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farringtonは1440年にイギリスランカシュアイアファリントンで生まれた。1464年にイギリスランカシュアイアでThomas Haworth of Farringtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington(은)는 1440년 영국 랭카실르 패링턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1464년 영국 랭카실르에서 Thomas Haworth of Farrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington nasceu em 1440, em Farington, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Thomas Haworth of Farrington em 1464, em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington родилась в 1440 г. в местности фарингтон, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Haworth of Farrington в 1464 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Jane "Haworth" of Farrington nació en 1440, en Farington, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Thomas Haworth of Farrington en 1464, en Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
